read.what_materialized: Forward plus backward hot-weight SDMA reaches the intended code path and completes six steps. It improves slightly over the backward-only SDMA run.
read.what_did_not_materialize: It still does not beat the non-SDMA route-mask/min-helper branch and remains far below the standard-EP top-8 hot-helper candidate.
read.why: The SDMA backend only changes the hot-weight all-reduce transport. The route-mask MORI/AITER branch still pays planning/masking, hot/normal row splitting, many tiny helper AITER fused-MoE calls, owner local VJP, and combine wait. The AITER logs still show helper calls with estimated_m_per_expert near 0-23, so helper-call granularity remains the dominant integration cost.
read.decision: Keep mori_sdma as an optional transport backend, but do not promote this branch. The next useful MORI path is batching/chunking/overlapping hot-weight movement with VJP or integrating helper hot-row execution into the normal MORI dispatch plus AITER fused expert path without the current tiny-call route-mask overhead.
